title: UMN TMCs -  Histology H&E Staining Protocol
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"l7uhx","text":"This SOP describes the procedure for Hematoxylin and Eosin (H\u0000\u0026E) staining, a widely used technique in histology for visualizing tissue structure. H\u0000\u0026E staining differentiates between cellular nuclei (stained blue) and cytoplasm/connective tissue (stained pink to red). The SOP ensures standardization of the H\u0000\u0026E staining process to produce high-quality, consistent results. Training on lab safety, chemical handling, and staining techniques is required for implementation.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"bihpv","text":"Training:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"h8stg","text":"1. Personnel must complete training on lab safety protocols, including the handling of hazardous chemicals under a fume hood and the use of PPE.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"hej4k","text":"2. Trainees will observe a trained technician, practice under supervision, and pass a practical test to demonstrate proficiency in the H\u0000\u0026E staining protocol.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"kd3u","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"28bju","text":"This protocol may require prior approval by the users' Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC) or equivalent ethics committee.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"9owjr","text":"Chemicals and Solutions:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":24}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5prwv","text":"- Harris Alum Hematoxylin","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"67fck","text":"- Acid Alcohol (70% alcohol + concentrated hydrochloric acid)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"jucbh","text":"- Ammonium Hydroxide (0.2% ammonia water)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"tofgm","text":"- Alcoholic Eosin","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"jm6e6","text":"- Xylene","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5y7bj","text":"- Ethanol (100%, 95%, 80%)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"xynuw","text":"- Glacial Acetic Acid","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"zg7qr","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1kweo","text":"Equipment:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":10}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ah5ts","text":"- 60°C oven","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"xvjph","text":"- Fume hood","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ytu6x","text":"- Personal protective equipment (PPE): goggles, gloves, mask","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Preparation of Slides:
Place slides in a 60°C oven to dry for 20 minutes.
Deparaffinization and Hydration:
Immerse slides in three changes of xylene, 5 minutes each.
Progress through two changes of 100% ethanol (3 minutes each), followed by 95% ethanol (3 minutes), and 80% ethanol (5 minutes).
Rinse slides in tap water for 5 minutes.
Hematoxylin Staining:
Stain slides with Harris hematoxylin solution for 5 minutes.
Rinse in running tap water.
Differentiation:
Dip slides in 1% acid alcohol for two quick dips to decolorize.
Bluing:
Dip slides in 0.2% ammonia water twice.
Rinse in running tap water for 5 minutes.
Dehydration and Counterstaining:
Place slides in 80% ethanol for 1 minute.
Counterstain with eosin Y working solution for 1 minute.
Final Dehydration and Clearing:
Dehydrate through one change of 80% ethanol (5 dips), two changes of 95% ethanol (8-10 dips each), and two changes of 100% ethanol (10-15 dips each).
Clear slides in two changes of xylene (15 dips each), then transfer to a third xylene bath for coverslipping.
Mounting:
Mount coverslips with a xylene-based medium.
creator: Allie Abdellatif
